name: Spencer Williams
description: American jazz and popular music composer, pianist, and singer (1889-1965)
abstract: Spencer Williams (October 14, 1889 – July 14, 1965) was an American jazz and popular music composer, pianist, and singer. He is best known for his hit songs "Basin Street Blues", "I Ain't Got Nobody", "Royal Garden Blues", "I've Found a New Baby", "Everybody Loves My Baby", "Tishomingo Blues", and many others.

works_created.t: Nobody in Town Can Bake a Sweet Jelly Roll Like Mine

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I met the members of Willy Lewis's band that were not with him when I met him in the south of France. ... Another musician and song writer that I had seen in New York who was now living in Paris was Spencer Williams

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Carew
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Spencer, yeah. Yeah, he was. Spencer was a pretty well-known piano player in New Orleans too, wasn't he? [INTERVIEWEE] I don't know.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] There were some records made by Spencer Williams and Teddy Bunn, piano and guitar. [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yeah?

about_quotes.speaker: Henry "Jack" Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: Spencer Williams. ... INTERVIEWEE: No, no. Clouse Williams was a clear, pretty piano player to me.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Schulz
about_quotes.evidence: bringing some of the earlier Louis Armstrong and King Oliver things out, and Spencer Williams tunes
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Schulz interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: Spencer Williams was gone... I never did get to know Steve Lewis... I never did get to meet him.
